scores.justification: Inherits romance's elite indie economics (44% of $10k+/mo earners write romance) via interconnected one-couple-per-book series that maximize KU read-through, with meritocratic also-bought discovery and a real white-space in suspense-forward/dark RS.

The cleanest zero-platform-to-empire proof (Mark Dawson, 6M+ copies) plus rising 2025 demand and a series-native recurring-operative structure; buildable if you target the action-espionage lane rather than credentialed tradecraft realism.

Over-indexes for pure-indie penetration (O'Mahoney, Ellsworth) on the back of long recurring-lawyer series; research is entirely public and free, and female/non-US/legal-tech leads are open white-space.

Best-in-class buildability (zero research, reader's own world) and an unlimited ceiling (The Housemaid), but standalone-heavy norms weaken automatic read-through and the trope conventions are near-mandatory.
The strongest indie series-read-through engine (recurring investigator, 10-20 books), but winning requires studio-level rapid-release cadence against saturated procedural competitors.

The most solo-friendly core-thriller lane — low research, format optionality (standalone or series), least studio-saturation and genuine setting/regional white-space — held back only by lacking a dedicated high-traffic bestseller node.
Best series economics (10-30 book recurring-hero runs) in a meritocratic KU space, but the Reacher-clone shelf is crowded and thriller under-earns romance, so it's an output-and-volume game.
The #1 MTS sub-genre with huge demand and low research burden, but it is the single most saturated lane where a no-platform debut cannot rank without narrow sub-category targeting.

Rewards a clever hidden-truth hook over credentials or partisanship (A.G. Riddle self-pubbed to 2M+ copies), with library-researchable premises, serial 'bigger revelation' structure and strong non-Western/science-driven white-space.
KU-native with strong series format, but it is the most defended, studio-saturated niche where a solo newcomer needs a stacked multi-book launch to survive, plus content-sensitivity risk.
Genuine low-saturation white-space and a 2026 trend tailwind, but demand is thin and fashion-cyclical, the top is trad-held, and the aesthetic-forward craft/cover bar is high for a first-timer.

Biggest topical AI/cyber white-space but highest research/expertise burden with softening core demand and no proven pure-indie franchise; only viable for a tech-literate author on the cyber sub-strain.

2024-26 reader fatigue, high authenticity burden, partisan-alienation risk, and a category over-supplied with trad A-listers yet thin in exploitable indie mid-list — the weakest indie fit unless hybridized toward conspiracy/action.

Steady demand and post-pandemic white-space, but the top is guarded by physician-credentialed authors and readers punish medical inaccuracy, giving it the highest expertise/gatekeeper dependence for a layperson.
Buzz is real but trad/BookTok-and-standalone-driven — the opposite of KU series economics — with genuinely hard fair-play plotting and the worst discoverability of the set for a no-platform author.

reasoning:
WINNER — Romantic-suspense / thriller-romance (8.65). For a solo indie with no platform, modest budget, and AI-assisted drafting, this is the single clearest path to meaningful income, and it wins precisely on the double-weighted lever (revenue) without being the most gatekept lane. It grafts thriller hooks onto romance's elite indie economics: romance is ~22.7% of indie authors' primary genre and, decisively, ~44% of authors earning $10k+/month write romance, with ~77% of top-100 romance titles in KU and extreme series loyalty. The native format — a team-of-heroes interconnected series pairing a new couple per book (Susan Stoker built a fully-indie NYT/USA Today/WSJ empire from 2014 on exactly this) — is engineered for the read-through that the KENP page-read economy rewards. Discoverability is meritocratic: romance readers find books through KU also-boughts, newsletter swaps and group promos rather than the BookTok/celebrity-club machine that gatekeeps locked-room and atmospheric standalones, so a cold-start author can still rank. Buildability is high — a moderate research burden (some procedural/protector flavor, far less than techno/medical/legal), cheap well-established cover conventions, no institutional access — and the white-space is real and current: non-military protectors (search-and-rescue, firefighters, PIs), older/own-voices heroines, and especially suspense-forward or 'dark' romantic suspense riding the dark-romance wave, a lane few pure-RS indies fully occupy. It scores strong-to-excellent on every axis (demand, revenue, series, buildability, low gatekeeper dependence) with only originality merely 'good' — which is exactly the balanced, non-saturated-at-the-top profile the brief asks for, rather than the biggest-but-brutally-crowded psychological/domestic center.

RUNNER-UP — Espionage (8.05, edging Legal and Domestic on the same score by its rising-demand tailwind and series-native structure). This is the strongest 'pure thriller' choice for a newcomer and the cleanest proof that a no-platform solo author can build an empire from zero: Mark Dawson started self-publishing John Milton in 2013 and reached 25+ books and 6M+ copies via a copyable playbook (recurring operative + reader-magnet funnel + mailing list + KU + $3.99–4.99 pricing), with Saul Herzog and others replicating it. Demand is the strongest and most clearly rising of the geopolitical cluster — 2025 was called a 'bumper year,' amplified by the Slow Horses / Night Agent screen halo — and the category is a tight, rankable Amazon node where a well-targeted launch can hit #1 in Espionage while still modest overall. Series potential is best-in-class (a single operative sustains 10-25+ installments plus spin-off siblings), which is the master revenue lever. Crucially, buildability is good IF the author picks the action-espionage lane rather than credentialed tradecraft-realism literary spy fiction: research is moderate and forgiving of propulsion over documentary accuracy, and no clearance or institutional access is required. White-space exists in female operatives, non-US/post-Soviet services, near-future cyber/AI espionage, and the morally-grey low-glamour 'Slow Horses' tone that is trad-dominated and thin on the indie KU shelf. It edges out the equally-scored Legal (deepest pure-indie penetration, best public-research buildability) and Small-town (the honorable mention for sheer solo-friendliness and freshest white-space, but weaker revenue ceiling and no dedicated bestseller node) on the combination of rising demand, proven zero-platform template, and series economics.
